children.name: Pluvianellus
children.description: A monotypic genus of enigmatic shorebird endemic to southern South America. The Magellanic Plover was long placed in the plover family Charadriidae but molecular studies confirm it is distinct enough to warrant its own family. It resembles a pale grey plover with a short, thick bill and red legs.

children.name: Pluvianellus socialis
children.description: A rare and localised shorebird of southern Patagonia and Tierra del Fuego, inhabiting the shores of shallow freshwater lakes and lagoons. It has a soft grey-and-white plumage with striking red legs and a black-tipped bill. Unlike most plovers it feeds by pecking at small invertebrates in mud rather than the characteristic run-stop-peck foraging pattern.
